video_title: Wait, They Put WHAT In That?! Top 10 Times Guy Fieri Was Shocked on #DDD 😱 | Food Network

restaurants.name: Lobster Shanty
restaurants.guy_intro: So that's it from the Lobster Shanty in Salem, Massachusetts.

dishes.description: Martini with lobster essence instead of vermouth, garnished with a lobster claw and lemon twist.
dishes.guy_response: Mm. Great lobster flavor. This is almost like the ghost of lobster martini. Yeah. You don't get the lobster. You just get the flavor. You just get the hint of it. I understand why you serve the claw with it. But you know what? I think for people that really like martinis and like them dirty, that's got it. It's absolutely lobster martini. It's fresh, and we've got some lemon in it. It's good. I'd definitely have it again. You never see it anywhere else.

restaurants.guy_intro: Crawfish cheesecake. What? Exactly. So a savory cheesecake.

dishes.description: Savory cheesecake with a Creole-seasoned breadcrumb crust, cream cheese, smoked gouda, Fontina, Romano, Parmesan, sauteed onions, garlic, green onions, lobster base, lemon juice, white pepper, and Louisiana crawfish tails. Served with Creole Meuniere sauce and hollandaise.
dishes.guy_response: It's delicious. That is so rich. And there is so much flavor. It's silky, great amount of cheese, perfectly seasoned. Good amount of the crawfish inside there. You're actually really happy when you get that little nugget. The sauces complement it incredibly well. The crust plays a pivotal role. That's my kind of cheesecake. I've got a crawfish cheesecake right here, baby.

dishes.description: Tongue sliced, seasoned with kosher salt and granulated garlic, breaded in flour and egg, fried, then simmered in a sauce with olive oil, crushed red pepper, onion, garlic puree, churroseta pepper sauce, salt, paprika, and tomato sauce.
dishes.guy_response: It's fantastic. The tongue, that is tender. It's like roast beef. The paprika, tomato, garlic sauce is off the chain. Yum, yum. Really good sauce.

restaurants.name: Cattleman's
restaurants.guy_intro: Brains and eggs. Cap brains and eggs, is that the specialty? It's just what we eat for breakfast every morning here at Cattleman's.

dishes.description: Brains cooked with scrambled eggs and steak seasoning.
dishes.guy_response: I don't like eggs, and I don't like brains. I definitely don't like egg and brains. Oh, god. I'm going to need toast. So the yellow part's the chicken, and the brown part is the brain matter. That's correct. You going to make me smarter? It's guaranteed to make you smarter. Can I have some sauce or something? There you go. Mind over matter, right? God, I don't want to do it!

restaurants.guy_intro: It's a lamb fry. Oh, awesome. Breaded lamb testicles.
I heard you're having the ear sandwich. It's really good. The spear, the ear. Let's get it on.

dishes.description: Pig ears seasoned with sugar, salt, cumin, paprika, chili powder, garlic, then slow-cooked in water and vinegar for about eight hours. Served on a sandwich with mustard and chopped onion.
dishes.guy_response: It's tender. It is tender. See the cartilage? Cooked down to a nice, tender state. It is very tasty. I would call it natural bologna. I didn't really think I was going to be too big of a fan. Once you're here, you love it.

restaurants.guy_intro: I've got the gator burger. I get to eat alligator burger, and you don't. What part of the alligator is this? Man, this is a solo.

dishes.description: Ground alligator meat (solo cut) formed into patties, seasoned with salt, pepper, paprika, and garlic powder, then grilled. Served with a gravy made from water, dried bananas, dried fruit (apples, apricots, plums), baby shrimp, and curry, finished with cream.
dishes.guy_response: I gotta tell you, I never thought I would really like it. Look at that. That's awesome. Great flavor to it. And the curry fruit thing, you know what? That's crazy. Actually tastes like a pork cutlet. It seems like all the flavors meld together to make one very fine burger.

restaurants.guy_intro: Roasted carrot pizza. Roasted carrot pizza is my absolute favorite dish. It's unexpected in the best way possible. That's what we're going to eat is a roasted carrot pizza? Yes, sir. All right, let's get into it, Chef.

dishes.description: Pizza with a high-gluten flour starter dough, topped with French soubise, Gruyere cheese, roasted carrots (seasoned with salt, pepper, orange zest, honey, butter, orange juice), and Fresno pepper. Garnished with Dukkah (Moroccan spice blend of almonds, sesame seeds, cumin, hazelnuts, black peppercorn, coriander, fennel, salt, pepper, paprika). Cooked in a wood-fired oven.
dishes.guy_response: Outstanding. The tang of the gruyere. The sweetness of the caramelized carrot. The spice of the Fresno. Without the duca, you sprinkle on, like, pixie dust. Wouldn't be a second. It's giving me goosebumps. That is probably in the top three most unique pizzas I've ever had in my life.

restaurants.guy_intro: Short ribs. They melt in your mouth. They're absolutely awesome. Dr. Pepper, yeah! Let's do this.

dishes.description: Boneless beef short ribs seasoned with salt and black pepper, seared, then braised in a hotel pan with mirepoix (carrot, celery, onion), fresh thyme, bay leaves, chipotle peppers in adobo sauce, homemade chicken stock, and Dr. Pepper for four hours at 350 degrees. Served with celery root puree, sautéed mustard greens, and fresh grated horseradish.
dishes.guy_response: The short rib is so tender that it's just falling apart. You just have to help it with the fork a little bit. The celery root puree is a great medium, the whole dish. The fresh horseradish, along with the mustard greens, adds a nice contrast to this braising liquid that's been reduced. And the Dr. Pepper, just this nice, round flavor, and mm, every little piece you did here, killer. I mean, really killer.

restaurants.guy_intro: The duck tongue tacos. This is play on traditional lengua with, like, beef tongue, but here, done with duck tongue. It does sound a little bit intimidating, but when you bite into it, it's all a perfect bite. So what are we making? Duck tongue tacos.

dishes.description: Duck tongues and duck neck seasoned with salt, onion, hot sauce, cilantro, peppercorns, garlic, and tortilla paste, then cooked in the oven for 3.5 hours at 300 degrees. Meat is shredded, cartilage removed, then caramelized with scallions and cilantro. Served in taco shells with chopped onions and queso fresco.
dishes.guy_response: Delicious. There's a depth of flavor that's coming through, one, because of this fantastic marinade that you made, two, with the richness of cooking something like the neck. I know it's a little tongue-in-cheek. Now that one, you've been sitting on it for a while. It's creative. The texture is great. The crispiness that you generate from that meat, to the tenderness that you get from the tongue, the acidic contrast that you get from the pickled onions, and then the little crunchy taco with it, that's some dynamite duck.

restaurants.guy_intro: Steak and cola, ready to finish, gents. The steak is really good. It's marinated with cola, which sounds weird at first, but once you try it, it's delicious. Steak and cola pizza.

dishes.description: Pizza with shredded beef marinated in Italian dressing, cola, soy sauce, granulated garlic, pepper, and salt. Topped with provolone, mozzarella, the marinated steak (raw), gorgonzola, and caramelized onions. Baked in the oven for eight minutes at 550 degrees.
dishes.guy_response: Right balance of cheese, and meat, and sauce. Not soggy. Bro, you got a pizza here. It's meaty, it's rich, and delivered on a crust that is up there with some of the best I've had. Chewy, yet tender. It's not overdone with the ingredients. The attention to detail is really paying off. Because I thought, it's either going to be a hero or a zero. That's a hero, that's a dynamite pie.
